creator: nwhitehorn@FreeBSD.org
text: The VGA driver has become unusably slow since r269471. I suspect that the support for non-8x8 fonts has dramatically increased the incidence of reads from VGA memory. It might be best if the driver could be refactored to avoid reads entirely, for example by passing some concept of write granularity up from the driver into the vt core so that vd_bitbltchr() is called only for operations of that width (redrawing partial characters).
